design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate labyrinthine levels, combat waves of enemies, manage ammunition/health/armor, reach exit or defeat boss
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource scarcity (ammo, health, armor) versus enemy volume and aggression
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Lone space marine fighting through demonic hordes with escalating firepower across Mars moons and Hell
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Action-heavy design prioritizing combat intensity over narrative (original story removed in final version). Emphasizes player agency through weapon variety and level exploration. Establishes FPS genre conventions: non-linear level design with keycards/switches, difficulty settings affecting enemy count/behavior, power-up drops from defeated enemies

difficulty.continues_system: Checkpoint-based respawning at level start after death
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Five selectable difficulty levels adjust enemy count and attack speed/damage; hardest difficulty increases enemy movement and attack velocity. Cheat codes available for player override (god mode, all weapons, walk-through-walls)

progression.upgrade_system: Weapon unlocks through level exploration and enemy drops (pistol → shotgun → chainsaw → plasma rifle → BFG 9000). Power-ups: health packs, armor, invulnerability, invisibility, radiation suit, berserker status, mapping computer
progression.unlock_mechanism: Keycards and remote switches gate progression; mandatory boss defeat at episode finale
replayability_hooks: Five difficulty levels with enemy variation
Non-linear level layout encourages exploration for weapons/ammo
Multiple episodes (three in shareware, four in retail 'Ultimate Doom')
Robust modding support and custom WADs

world_building_techniques: Environmental design: Mars military bases → Hell (visual progression)
Enemy types suggest demonic invasion and human possession
Level naming ('Knee-Deep in the Dead', episode titles) provides minimal context
Opening crawl/manual provides setup; in-game narrative nearly absent

boss_design.philosophy: Single boss encounter at episode finale; designed as culmination of level's combat patterns. Boss presents volumetric threat requiring ammo management and dodging ability

faction_indicators: Mars military establishment vs. Hell forces (spatial narrative)
Player as lone marine survivor (solitary faction of one)
danger_signals: Demon presence escalation from Mars to Hell
Boss encounters as territorial markers of demonic control
environmental_storytelling: Military base architecture (Mars episodes) transitioning to hellscape
Toxic waste pits and crushing mechanisms signaling hazardous environment
Enemy placement patterns suggest demonic strongholds
Locked doors and keycard systems imply military facility hierarchy disruption
